title: Social Security Numbers exposed in the NDH bulk export
headline: 63 confirmed SSN exposures + 0 date-of-birth exposures + 21 NPI-as-name data-integrity violations in the 2026-04-09 NDH bulk export Practitioner resources, independently verifying and extending the 2026-04-30 Washington Post finding. SSN breakdown: 42 dashed in qualification[].identifier[].value (state-license slot), 4 dashed in name[].given[], 0 dashed in name[].family, 17 undashed-9-digit in name[].given[], 0 undashed in name[].family. 5 international phone-format false positives filtered out. 2 Organization resources also carry SSN-pattern strings.
notes: Independently verifies the 2026-04-30 Washington Post finding by scanning the 2026-04-09 NDH bulk export (already loaded into BigQuery as `cms_npd.practitioner`/`cms_npd.organization`) for the dashed SSN format \\d{3}-\\d{2}-\\d{4} in the full resource JSON. WaPo reported 'dozens'; the AINPI scan identifies 63 confirmed exposures across 24 states. CMS attributed the leak to 'incorrect entries of provider or provider-representative-supplied information in the wrong places' — borne out by the JSON-location breakdown: most SSNs are in qualification.identifier.value (the state-license slot), with 4 cases of providers entering their SSN literally as a name token. Privacy posture: AINPI publishes counts, JSON locations, NPIs (professional IDs, not PII), and state breakdowns. The SSN values themselves are NOT republished in this finding's output, even though they remain in the public NDH bulk file CMS distributed. State Medicaid PI teams that want to validate or remediate should contact CMS NDH operations directly.
